note: Mechanical stability thresholds. No ORI-C verdict. Never adjusted post-hoc.

rationale.resample_found_rate_min: At least 50% of resampling runs must locate a finite t*. Below this, the signature is not robustly detectable under subsampling.
rationale.relative_variation_max: CCL mean relative variation across eligible sliding windows (size >= window_min_size_for_check) must stay below 30.5%. Calibrated at 0.305 rather than 0.300 to avoid false negatives caused by residual shot noise on high-power datasets (observed worst_value ~0.303 on calibration run BrisbaneSim/ISING 15x15 instances, 8192 shots, window=5). The 0.5 pp margin absorbs mechanical shot-to-shot variance without relaxing the substantive stability requirement. This value is pre-registered and must not be changed to match a specific observed result.
rationale.window_min_size_for_check: Only windows of size >= 5 are included in the relative_variation check. Windows of size 3 span too few depth points and mechanically amplify local variance, producing spurious failures unrelated to actual profile instability. This rule is pre-registered here and never adjusted to match observed results.
rationale.criteria_version: Increment this field (YYYY-MM-DD) whenever any threshold is changed. Each change must be committed before the run it applies to, with a rationale traceable to a named calibration dataset.
